mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate above 0.5pp signals tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in low UNRATE regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or UNRATE declines correlate negatively with returns (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP and housing starts signal weak demand, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 falls > 1.0% q/q and HOUST falls > 2.0% m/m
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in demand contraction scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or individual proxies show no directional correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production above 1.5% signals strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO expansion periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or INDPRO trends show no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI above 2.5% triggers inflation fears, prompting monetary tightening and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high CPI environments are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or CPI shows no directional correlation (Rank IC < -0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 6-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) below 350 billion signals weak consumer demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in RSAFS contraction periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or RSAFS trends show no predictive power (Rank IC < -0.04).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in payroll employment and rising unemployment signal labor market deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls > 50k and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in labor market deterioration scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or individual proxies show no directional correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production above 1.0% combined with falling capacity utilization signals overheating supply chains, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0% in 3 months and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in supply expansion scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction terms show no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).
